verses.text: Let servants honour their masters, 'that the name of God... be not blasphemed.' Honest service. The believer's work commends the gospel.
Let servants honour their masters, 'that the name of God... be not blasphemed.' Honest service. The believer's work commends the gospel.
From teachers of strife, 'supposing that gain is godliness,' withdraw thyself. Beware greed. False teachers prize profit over truth.
From teachers of strife, 'supposing that gain is godliness,' withdraw thyself. Beware greed. False teachers prize profit over truth.
From teachers of strife, 'supposing that gain is godliness,' withdraw thyself. Beware greed. False teachers prize profit over truth.
'Godliness with contentment is great gain'; having food and raiment, 'let us be therewith content.' True wealth. Contentment with God is riches indeed.
'Godliness with contentment is great gain'; having food and raiment, 'let us be therewith content.' True wealth. Contentment with God is riches indeed.
'Godliness with contentment is great gain'; having food and raiment, 'let us be therewith content.' True wealth. Contentment with God is riches indeed.
They that will be rich fall into a snare; 'the love of money is the root of all evil.' Money's danger. Craving riches pierces the soul with many sorrows.
They that will be rich fall into a snare; 'the love of money is the root of all evil.' Money's danger. Craving riches pierces the soul with many sorrows.
Flee these things; 'fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life.' Press on. The man of God pursues holiness and life.
Flee these things; 'fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life.' Press on. The man of God pursues holiness and life.
Keep this charge till His appearing; He is 'the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings.' Sovereign Lord. He alone has immortality and glory.
Keep this charge till His appearing; He is 'the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings.' Sovereign Lord. He alone has immortality and glory.
Keep this charge till His appearing; He is 'the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings.' Sovereign Lord. He alone has immortality and glory.
Keep this charge till His appearing; He is 'the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings.' Sovereign Lord. He alone has immortality and glory.
Charge the rich not to trust 'in uncertain riches,' but to do good, laying up 'a good foundation.' Rich in works. The wealthy must store treasure in heaven.
Charge the rich not to trust 'in uncertain riches,' but to do good, laying up 'a good foundation.' Rich in works. The wealthy must store treasure in heaven.
Charge the rich not to trust 'in uncertain riches,' but to do good, laying up 'a good foundation.' Rich in works. The wealthy must store treasure in heaven.
O Timothy, keep that committed to thee, avoiding 'profane and vain babblings.' Guard the deposit. Hold fast the truth and shun empty talk.
O Timothy, keep that committed to thee, avoiding 'profane and vain babblings.' Guard the deposit. Hold fast the truth and shun empty talk.
